name: sim-flow-vscode
description: VS Code adapter for the sim-flow AI-assisted modeling orchestrator (DMF and DSF).

chatParticipants.name: sim-flow
chatParticipants.description: Drive the AI-assisted modeling flow (DMF and DSF).

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show the current flow, step, and gate status.

commands.name: runs
commands.description: List recent experiment runs. Optional filter args are forwarded to the CLI.

commands.name: gate
commands.description: Read-only gate validation for <step>. Defaults to the current step.

commands.name: advance
commands.description: Validate the gate and, if clean, mark the step passed and bump current_step.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset a step and cascade downstream gates. Usage: /reset <step>.

commands.name: step
commands.description: Start a work or critique session. Usage: /step <step-id>.work or /step <step-id>.critique.

commands.name: auto
commands.description: Run the entire flow unattended from the current step through the end (work -> critique -> advance per step). Optional --spec <path> seeds DM0; otherwise DM0.work is interactive and the rest is automated.

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize sim-flow state in the current workspace.

commands.title: sim-flow: Run Flow (CLI agent in terminal)

sim-flow.binaryPath.description: Absolute path to the sim-flow binary. Empty string falls back to $PATH, then to the bundled binary.

sim-flow.foundationRoot.description: Explicit path to the sim-foundation repository root. Passed to the CLI as --foundation-root. Empty string delegates to the CLI's own resolution.

enumDescriptions: Spawn a fresh CLI-agent session per step (per-step mode). User types `/exit` to end each step; orchestrator gates and advances automatically.

Keep one CLI-agent session alive for the whole flow (single-session mode). Dashboard buttons inject prompts / gate results / advance commands via a Unix control socket. User types `/exit` to end the entire flow; orchestrator lazy-respawns on the next dashboard command.
sim-flow.session.mode.description: Lifecycle for the interactive CLI-agent backend (`claude-cli` etc.). Ignored for chat-pane backends.

sim-flow.llm.verbose.description: When OFF, the dashboard injects a `be concise` system message into every LLM request so models skip preamble, recaps, and hedging language. ON keeps the model's natural verbosity.

enumDescriptions: Use VS Code's built-in Language Model API (requires Copilot or another provider).
Call the Anthropic API directly; requires an API key in SecretStorage.
Call the OpenAI API directly; requires an API key in SecretStorage.
Call a local Ollama server via its OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
Call a local LM Studio server via its OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
Anthropic Claude CLI (terminal-only). Runs `sim-flow auto --llm-backend claude` in a VS Code terminal; uses the user's `claude /login` (Pro/Team).
OpenAI Codex CLI (terminal-only). Runs `sim-flow auto --llm-backend codex` in a VS Code terminal; uses the user's `codex login`.
GitHub Copilot CLI (terminal-only). Runs `sim-flow auto --llm-backend gh-copilot` in a VS Code terminal; uses the user's `gh auth login`.
sim-flow.llm.source.description: Which language model source to use. The first five run inside the @sim-flow chat pane; the `*-cli` variants run `sim-flow auto` in a terminal tab and bypass the chat participant -- pick one when you want to use your existing CLI auth instead of API keys.

enumDescriptions: Each /step launches a fresh chat tab; the tab closes when the session ends.
Sticky tab per project; new sessions stream into the existing tab.
sim-flow.chat.scope.description: Reserved for Phase 9 polish; currently the chat-tab lifecycle follows VS Code's defaults.

sim-flow.llm.model.description: Model identifier passed to the configured source. Empty string delegates to the source's default.

sim-flow.llm.ollama.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Include the `/v1` suffix.

sim-flow.llm.lmstudio.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the LM Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Include the `/v1` suffix.

sim-flow.dashboard.openOnActivate.description: Automatically open the Flow Dashboard when a sim-flow project is detected in the workspace.

sim-flow.dashboard.showFullyAutomated.description: Show the red end-to-end automated-flow button next to the manual Play / Stop controls. Hidden by default because the automated flow walks every step (DM0 → DM4b) without stopping for review and can burn significant LLM credits. Toggle this on (Settings tab) only when you genuinely want a hands-off run from a complete spec.

sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimEnabled.description: When the Generate Verilog button runs, also instruct the agent to simulate the emitted RTL with the user-supplied simulator binary, diagnose any failures, and iterate the generated SystemVerilog until simulation matches the Foundation model. Requires `sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimulatorPath` to be non-empty; otherwise the simulate-and-iterate section is omitted from the injected prompt.

sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimulatorPath.description: Absolute path (or PATH-resolvable command) of a SystemVerilog simulator the agent may invoke when `sim-flow.dashboard.verilogSimEnabled` is true. Examples: `/usr/local/bin/verilator`, `/tools/synopsys/vcs/bin/vcs`, `/opt/modelsim/bin/vsim`. Leave empty to disable the simulate-and-iterate appendix even when the checkbox is on.

sim-flow.auto.maxWorkIterations.description: Per-session structural-gate iteration cap in automated mode. If a single work or critique session can't reach a clean structural gate within this many turns, the orchestrator falls through to interactive.

sim-flow.auto.maxCritiqueIterations.description: Maximum number of times the auto driver re-runs a step's work session in response to critique blockers before stopping the auto run.

sim-flow.debug.description: Debug log categories. Each enabled token writes to <project>/.sim-flow/logs/. `events` = parsed events both directions plus the full message stack on every LLM request. `raw` = JSONL lines on the wire. `llm` = vscode.lm dispatch detail (input messages, every chunk, errors). Empty disables logging. Setting overrides the SIM_FOUNDATION_DEBUG environment variable.

scripts.compile: npm run codegen:protocol && npm run typecheck:extension && npm run compile:extension && npm run compile:webview-types && npm run compile:webview
scripts.ci: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm run test
